question: Will CPI rise more than 0.9% in March 2026?
reasoning: The March 2026 CPI report was officially released by the Bureau of Labor Statistics on April 10, 2026. The data shows CPI increased only 0.3% in March 2026 on a seasonally adjusted basis, well below the 0.9% threshold required for YES resolution. Since the official BLS data is final and authoritative, there is zero probability this market resolves YES.
key_factors: Official BLS March 2026 CPI data released showing 0.3% monthly increase
Market requires >0.9% increase, actual was only 0.3%
BLS data is final and definitive for market resolution
Market closes April 10, 2026 - same day as data release
risks: Potential data revision (though CPI revisions are rare and typically minor)
Misinterpretation of seasonally vs non-seasonally adjusted figures
Market resolution criteria ambiguity regarding decimal precision
